nodes.title: Research & Evaluation Methods
nodes.description: Conceptualization, collection and analysis of primary and secondary data; interpretation, and communication of results of applied research and/or evaluation studies for decision makers. Prerequisite: Three hours of Statistics. Cross-listed with: CDAE 6510.
nodes.other_requirements: Three hours of Statistics. Must register for lab PA 6030 OL1; Cross listed with CDAE 6510; Total combined enrollment: 25; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Public Administration Capstone
nodes.description: The Capstone is designed to provide Master of Public Administration students with a summative experience that ties learning competencies to evidence drawn from their course of study. Pre/co-requisites: Core Master of Public Administration courses either prior to Spring semester or simultaneously.
nodes.other_requirements: Core Master of Public Administration courses either prior to Spring semester or simultaneously. Graduate students only

nodes.title: Policy Systems
nodes.description: The study and application of system-level public policy frameworks, theories and models to contemporary policy problems and solutions.

nodes.title: State and Local Government
nodes.description: For advanced students within areas of expertise of the faculty. Varied course offerings. Contemporary topics. Instructor Permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor Permission. Asynchronous Course. Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: US Elect Admin:Challenges/Pros
nodes.description: Current issues and new developments in public policy and public administration. Prerequisite: Permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Permission. Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Internship
nodes.description: Supervised administrative experience culminating in a written report.

nodes.title: Human Resource Management
nodes.description: Provides an in-depth exploration of human resource management (HRM) within public and nonprofit organizations, examining both its strategic and operational dimensions. Investigate how HRM influences organizational capacity, decision-making, and the achievement of mission-driven goals in an increasingly complex workforce environment. Connects human resources (HR) theory to the realities of managing people in public service, emphasizing public administration principles like equity, accountability, and ethical leadership.

nodes.title: Org Theory & Behavior
nodes.description: Examination of basic classical and contemporary theory, research on human relations, internal structures, environments, types, diverse workplaces, general properties of complex organizations and bureaucracies.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Public and Nonprofit Budgeting
nodes.description: A focus on the budget as the primary policy and planning document in public and nonprofit organizations.

nodes.title: Policy Analysis&Program Eval
nodes.description: A seminar providing hands-on knowledge in policy analysis and program evaluation using case studies of current analysis projects and problems. Specific techniques include planning, survey administration, forecasting, cost benefit analysis, and impact assessment.

nodes.title: Community Economic Development
nodes.description: Examines how rural and urban communities address poverty, unemployment and other economic problems through job creation and retention, workforce training and support, and other development strategies. Cross-listed with: CDAE 6260.

nodes.title: Systems Anly & Strategic Mgmt
nodes.description: The manifestations of resilience thinking across disciplines often looks different in theory and practice. In this course, students will focus on that nexus by exploring how community resilience is understood and operationalized across different and complex contexts, including public-private partnerships, intergovernmental arrangements, and strategic alliances. Students will leave the course with concrete understanding of resilience concepts, and an understanding of how to apply and leverage those concepts as tools via strategic management.

nodes.title: Foundations of Public Admin
nodes.description: Survey of major elements of management in the public and nonprofit sectors with special attention given to problems arising from political imperatives generated by a democratic society.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Master's Thesis Research
nodes.description: Research for the Master's Thesis. Thesis topic must be approved by faculty advisor.
nodes.other_requirements: Thesis topic must be approved by faculty advisor. Instructor permission required
